rulings.Case Title: NIMER A MASSIS VS. AYMAN A NESHEIWAT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 17, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT AYMAN NESHEIWAT, MARILYN ZEIDAN's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendants Nesheiwat and Zeidan's motion to vacate the default entered on August 23, 2019 is granted. Defendants have moved for relief within a reasonable time, lodged a proposed answer, and demonstrate mistake and/or excusable neglect. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 473(b).) There is a liberal policy to hear cases on the merits. Defendants were acting in pro per when they were served with the second amended complaint. The second amended complaint did not include a summons warning defendants that they needed to answer again. It was not unreasonable for defendants, who are not versed in the law, to believe that their answer to the first amended complaint would constitute a sufficient response to the second amended complaint. Defendants shall remit $1,000 in fees to plaintiff by December 1, 2019 as reasonable fees for obtaining the default. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 473(c)(1)(C); Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 823.) Defendants shall file their answer by October 25, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE WEST VS. DAVID PERALTA JR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: OCTOBER 17, 2019 Line 1, PLAINTIFF INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE WEST Notice Of Motion To Compel Pro Tem Judge Peter Vestal, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Cont'd to October 31, 2019 per the agreement of the parties. Email dated 10/15/19 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pvestal@nvlawllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL VS. MELVIN LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joint Motion And Joint Motion For Leave Of Court To File Second Amended Verified Answer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 17, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANT TERESA WONG, VAN NESS CARE CENTER, INC., SAN FRANCISCO CARE CENTER, L.P., MEL LEE MANAGEMENT, INC.'s Joint Motion For Leave Of Court To File Second Amended Verified Answer. Defendants' joint motion for leave of Court to file second amended verified answer is granted on the conditions that Defendants re-open the individual depositions of Melvin Lee and Teresa Wong related to the new affirmative defenses in the second amended verified answer and serve substantive responses to Plaintiffs' discovery requests related to the new affirmative defenses within 30 days of service of such requests. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. The conditions on the granting of the motion avoid any legal prejudice to Plaintiffs by the granting of the motion. (Code Civ. Proc. §§ 473(a)(1), 576 [court is authorized to grant leave "on such terms as may be proper"].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL VS. MELVIN LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Joint Mtn And Joint Mtn For Leave Of Court To File X-Cplt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 17, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANT MELVIN LEE, TERESA WONG, VAN NESS CARE CENTER, INC., SAN FRANCISCO CARE CENTER, L.P., MEL LEE MANAGEMENT, INC.'s Joint Motion For Leave Of Court To File Cross-Complaint. Defendants' joint motion for leave of Court to file cross-complaint is granted on the conditions that Defendants re-open the individual depositions of Melvin Lee and Teresa Wong related to any new allegations in the cross-complaint and serve substantive responses to the City's discovery requests related to the allegations in the cross-complaint within 30 days of service of such requests. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. The conditions on the granting of the motion avoid any legal prejudice to Plaintiffs by the granting of the motion. (Code Civ. Proc. § 426.50 [court is authorized to grant leave "upon such terms as may be just to the parties"]; Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 101 [court may mitigate any prejudice by "postponement or other conditions to prevent injustice"].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SAMER NASSER ET AL VS. LISA MICHELLE MCCORMICK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 17, 2019, Line 15, DEFENDANT LISA MCCORMICK, PAUL STEELY, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Lisa Michelle McCormick, Estate of Robert McCormick, Steely McCormick, Paul Charles Steely, and Michelle McCormick's demurrer to Plaintiffs Samer Nasser and Kelly Graff's second amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiffs may plead inconsistent allegations in the alternative. (Mendoza v. Continental Sales Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.) Plaintiffs sufficiently plead a general negligence cause of action against Defendants by alleging, in the alternative, either Defendant Michelle McCormick (a.k.a. Lisa Michelle McCormick), Defendant Robert McCormick, or Defendant Steely McCormick (a.k.a. Paul Charles Steely) was the driver and/or possessor of the vehicle at the time of the alleged car accident. (See 2d. Am. Compl. p. 6-8 and Ex. 1.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SAMER NASSER ET AL VS. LISA MICHELLE MCCORMICK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 17, 2019, Line 15, DEFENDANT LISA MCCORMICK, PAUL STEELY, ESTATE OF ROBERT MCCORMICK's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint. Defendants Lisa Michelle McCormick, Estate of Robert McCormick, Steely McCormick, Paul Charles Steely, and Michelle McCormick's motion to strike portions of Plaintiffs Samer Nasser and Kelly Graff's second amended complaint is granted. Paragraphs MV-2(d) and (e) on page 4 are stricken from the second amended complaint. There are no facts alleged in the complaint to support either a cause of action of negligent entrustment or agency theory. Besides alleging all Defendants are family members, Plaintiffs fail to allege ultimate facts support an agency theory to hold Defendants vicariously liable. (See Doe v. Roman Catholic Archbishop of Los Angeles (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 953, 969; cf. Moore v. Regents of University of California (1990) 51 Cal.3d 120, 134 fn.12 [criticizing similar secondary-liability allegations as "egregious examples of generic boilerplate"].) Furthermore, Plaintiffs state they meant to remove the negligent entrustment cause of action from the second amended complaint. (Opp. p. 3.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MOSAICA 601 HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION A CALIFORNIA VS. CALIFORNIA HOMESTEAD ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 17, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA HOMESTEAD ASSOCIATION, JAMES E. ROBERTS-OBAYASHI CORPORATION's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint. Defendant and Cross-Complainants California Homestead Association and James E. Roberts-Obayashi Corporation's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted. (Code Civ. Proc. §§ 426.50, 428.50(c); Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99 [even on "eve of trial," leave to file compulsory cross-complaint is mandatory absent bad faith].) Plaintiff has not shown that Defendants are acting in bad faith or have unduly delayed to its prejudice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)
